briefing.headline: Rig up a Lipless Crankbait — finesse pre-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Lipless Crankbait is a strong choice for pre-spawn with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: Fish the Red Eye Shad like a worm or jig, not just cast-and-reel. Rip it out of cover, let it flutter down — that swimming shimmy on the fall is the magic. The colder it is, the slower you fish. Downsize to 1/4oz on ultra-shallow flats (1-2ft). Upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the fall and

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: Fish the Red Eye Shad like a worm or jig, not just cast-and-reel. Rip it out of cover, let it flutter down — that swimming shimmy on the fall is the magic. The colder it is, the slower you fish.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, flat, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: In extreme cold on grass lakes, upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the bait down. Downsize to quarter or 3/8 oz for ultra-shallow flats. Fish it like a jig — short hopping, not just cast and r
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, flat, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Robertson: During prespawn at Chickamauga, fish an offshore pattern with a lipless crankbait in grass, then go headhunting with a big swimbait for kicker bites.

KVD: Fish the lipless crankbait like a jig in cold water. Short hop it along the bottom. Pull it around cover, deflect off rocks. Rip it out of grass, let it flutter down. Use rod tip to manipulate depth — tip up in shallow, down to get deeper. Ma...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal pre-spawn fish pull back to 6-12ft staging areas. They won't commit shallow after the front. Target the first breakline off spawning flats and key on deeper structure near shallow spawning zones.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
